title: Maximum Sum Rectangle in Matrix

content.problemStatement:
Given an M×NM×NM×N matrix of integers, find the rectangle (submatrix) that has the maximum sum of its elements. The rectangle can be of any size from 1×11×11×1 up to M×NM×NM×N.


Input Format

First line contains TTT, number of test cases
For each test case:

First line contains MMM and N,N,N, dimensions of matrix
Next MMM lines contain NNN space-separated integers each




Output Format

For each test case, print the maximum sum possible from any rectangle in the matrix.
content.inputFormat: First line contains T, the number of test cases. For each test case, the first line contains two integers M and N, the number of rows and columns of the matrix. Then M lines follow, each containing N space‑separated integers.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single integer – the maximum possible sum of any rectangular sub‑matrix.

sampleTestCases.explanation: The rectangle formed by rows 2‑3 and columns 2‑4 (0‑based) gives sum 29, which is maximal.
Test case 1: all numbers are positive, so the whole matrix sum is 4. Test case 2: all numbers are negative, the best we can do is pick the largest element -1.
All entries are negative; the maximum sum rectangle is the single element -1.

solution.approach: Fix the top row of the rectangle and expand the bottom row one by one. For each pair of rows we compress the matrix columns into a 1‑D array where each entry is the sum of elements between the two rows in that column. The best rectangle for this row pair is then the maximum subarray sum of the compressed array, which can be found with Kadane's algorithm in O(N). Repeating for all O(M^2) pairs of rows gives an overall O(M^2·N) solution, fast enough for the given limits. The algorithm works for all‑negative matrices because Kadane returns the largest (least negative) element.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N) extra space for the temporary column sums.

code.Java: import java.io.*;import java.util.*;public class Main {private static int kadane(int[] arr){int maxSoFar=arr[0];int cur=arr[0];for(int i=1;i<arr.length;i++){cur=Math.max(arr[i],cur+arr[i]);maxSoFar=Math.max(maxSoFar,cur);}return maxSoFar;}public static void main(String[] args) throws Exception{BufferedReader br=new BufferedReader(new InputStreamReader(System.in));StringBuilder sb=new StringBuilder();int T=Integer.parseInt(br.readLine().trim());while(T-- >0){StringTokenizer st=new StringTokenizer(br.readLine());int M=Integer.parseInt(st.nextToken());int N=Integer.parseInt(st.nextToken());int[][] mat=new int[M][N];for(int i=0;i<M;i++){st=new StringTokenizer(br.readLine());for(int j=0;j<N;j++){mat[i][j]=Integer.parseInt(st.nextToken());}}int best=Integer.MIN_VALUE;int[] temp=new int[N];for(int top=0;top<M;top++){Arrays.fill(temp,0);for(int bottom=top;bottom<M;bottom++){for(int col=0;col<N;col++){temp[col]+=mat[bottom][col];}best=Math.max(best,kadane(temp));}}sb.append(best).append('\n');}System.out.print(sb.toString());}}
